items.title: Telehealth Abortion Is Still Possible Without Mifepristone
items.summary: Courts may restrict access to the popular abortion medication mifepristone in the United States. Telehealth providers have backup plans in place.

items.title: The Italian Dubbing of ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ Has Stirred Up a Surprising Controversy
items.summary: The voice actors from the original film have returned for the sequel—and not everyone is happy about it.

items.title: Pornhub Restores Access for UK Adults Who Use Apple’s Age Verification
items.summary: The porn giant blocked its content from new UK users in February but says device-based age verification is more secure than third-party sites.

items.title: Altara secures $7M to bridge the data gap that’s slowing down physical sciences
items.summary: Altara’s AI aims to diagnose failures and help speed up R&D by unifying data siloed across spreadsheets and legacy systems.

items.title: Lucid Motors doesn’t know how many EVs it will build this year
items.summary: Lucid Motors pulled its guidance for the year, as it navigates swelling inventory and a companywide cost-cutting measure.

items.title: As crypto cools, a16z crypto raises a $2.2B fund
items.summary: As some of the biggest VCs in crypto start to consider funding AI startups, a16z crypto's new fund will stay the course.

items.title: Offensive stage of Iran war is over, US Secretary of State Rubio says
items.summary: As a spate of incidents in the Strait of Hormuz test the ceasefire, an Iranian official says, "We are just getting started".

items.title: Hantavirus may have spread between passengers on cruise ship, WHO says
items.summary: Two cases of the virus, which rarely spreads between humans, have been confirmed on the ship, and three people have died.

items.title: Russian attacks kill more than 20 ahead of rival ceasefires proposed by Kyiv and Moscow
items.summary: Kyiv said it would begin a truce on 6 May and then "act symmetrically" after Moscow declared a pause for its Victory Day parade.

items.title: Intel and Micron are poised to break major milestones
items.summary: Options traders can't get enough of semiconductor stocks.

items.title: Anthropic CEO warns of cyber ‘moment of danger’ as AI exposes thousands of vulnerabilities
items.summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei warned AI has created a narrow window for software firms, governments and banks to fix tens of thousands of vulnerabilities.

items.title: SEC advances Trump-backed proposal to end mandatory quarterly earnings reports
items.summary: The SEC formally proposed a rule change that would allow companies to file semiannual reports on a new form, 10-S, in place of the traditional quarterly10-Qs

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.summary: Eric Trump, President Trump’s son, unveiled the new logo for Palm Beach International Airport (PBI), which will soon be renamed after his father. The younger Trump posted a photo of the logo to the social platform X. It features a golden eagle, donning a crest shield with stars and stripes, holding two olive branches. Under…

items.title: Rubio says War Powers Act ‘100 percent unconstitutional’
items.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Tuesday argued the War Powers Act is unconstitutional, adding that the Trump administration is only complying with “elements” of it to maintain good relations with Congress. “The War Powers Act is unconstitutional, 100 percent,” Rubio told reporters during a White House briefing, claiming that his position has been shared…

items.title: Trump, allies aim to tame Iran rhetoric as war grows unpopular
items.summary: President Trump and his administration officials are sending signals to the MAGA base that they want to calm the waters when it comes to rhetoric on Iran as the war grows unpopular with Republicans and the general public ahead of the midterms.  With the war wreaking havoc on energy prices, Trump, along with Defense Secretary…

items.summary: BitMine Reports 5.18 Million ETH Holdings as Staking Revenue Reaches $297 Million

items.title: Breakthrough biomaterial heals tissue from the inside out
items.summary: Scientists have developed a breakthrough injectable biomaterial that travels through the bloodstream to repair damaged tissue from within, reducing inflammation and jumpstarting healing. In animal studies, it successfully treated heart attack damage and even showed promise for conditions like traumatic brain injury and pulmonary hypertension. Unlike earlier approaches that required direct injection into the heart, this new therapy can be delivered intravenously, allowing it to spread evenly and act quickly.

items.title: Scientists say travel could slow aging and boost your health
items.summary: A new study suggests travel could be a surprisingly powerful anti-aging tool. By viewing tourism through the lens of entropy, researchers found that positive travel experiences may help the body stay balanced and resilient. Activities like exploring new places, staying active, and connecting with others can boost immunity, metabolism, and stress recovery. However, stressful or unsafe travel could reverse these benefits.

items.title: Scientists connect “time crystal” to real device in quantum breakthrough
items.summary: A strange kind of matter that “ticks” forever without energy input has just taken a major leap toward real-world use. Known as a time crystal, this quantum system repeats its motion endlessly—like a clock that never winds down—and scientists have now managed to connect it to an external device for the first time. By linking the time crystal to a tiny mechanical oscillator, researchers showed they can actually control its behavior, opening the door to powerful new technologies.

items.title: What to know about a rare hantavirus outbreak at sea
items.summary: Public health officials are racing to find out how the sometimes deadly hantavirus got aboard a cruise ship and if there has been human-to-human spread.

items.title: Do GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic prevent cancer?
items.summary: Several studies have served up tantalizing hints about the drugs’ potential cancer prevention benefits, but other results land all over the map.

items.title: A small object past Pluto may have a thin atmosphere
items.summary: A brief stellar eclipse suggests the tiny 2002 XV93 has a thin atmosphere — a first for any solar system body farther from the sun than Pluto.
